## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hello and welcome to the template part of the training. My name is Saba, I work with Stefan and I made quite a few of these templates so I wanted to walk you through how these work and how you can basically apply them to your funnel so that you increase your chances of conversions, fix any major areas or elements that are obviously not working for your page or emails or ads and we'll walk through each of these step by step. So today we're going to go through the landing pages section. This is, we've got five different styles of landing pages and one thing I want you to remember when you're working with these is make sure that you're not following, you don't have to follow them exactly as they're outlined here. These are definitely guidelines for you to sort of understand what elements need to be on the page. You can change the order of these if you want to. You can test it as is but definitely install some sort of page tracking like a hot jar or a crazy egg, just the heat maps to see where people are clicking, what they're paying the most attention to and then optimize accordingly. So let's get started with this supplement or e-com landing page. This is a, I would say this is a hybrid of a sort of a product description page and a long form sales page. So this is where you can use longer blocks of copy versus just doing sort of bullet points and very short, a very short form. So I'll walk you through this and then I'll show you a variation where it's a lot shorter. This is probably the longest one but essentially each of these templates is going to have these things in brackets and the items in brackets are going to be the things that are at the different sections. So up here you want to have a logo, you have the attention bar or banner and we've given you some examples of what you could write for copy and of course you don't have to use these word for word, they're just sort of like prompts if you're stuck on what the copy should look like or what it should sound like then these are some good sort of guidelines to follow. So then you have the headline, you can use things like how a trusted authority, how doctors get away with whatever it is. There's different ways obviously to write your headline. So these are just prompts that you can use. Subhead, this is where you would make a promise, a big promise related to the headline. The core benefits, you can do three to five, sometimes six, it can be a grid but the key benefits being things like only one spoon a day to get results, results in 30 minutes, whatever it is, whatever claims or key benefits you have, you can call them out here. Sort of as bullet points, if somebody was scanning, think of all of these as if somebody was just scanning the page, how much information could they absorb just with that scan versus reading everything word for word, that's the general idea of these. The headline related to the new discovery, this is an idea as well, you don't have to use this headline, just make sure you have some sort of headline in here to break up

## [03:00 - 06:02]

the page. And again, here's some more examples, like this could be a block of text and this will most likely be a much longer block of text than what we have here. This is an example for you with some prompts in it but your copy is probably going to be about three or four paragraphs long so this box, don't limit yourself to the boxes that we've created here, just use them as prompts and then fill them out with as much information as you want to. Here's what people are saying, testimonials are definitely a big deal on all e-com side pages, ideally you want to sprinkle them throughout the page and sometimes you'll notice that some pages even start with the testimonial, the testimonial might actually be the attention bar so that's where somebody will say something like five stars and then they're a little blurb or you could say thousands of five star reviews, things like that. Idea being that a lot of people coming to these pages are in the process of making a buying decision and so anything you can do to sort of strengthen their decision is social proof is a big one. So if you can use a testimonial, thousands, hundreds of five star reviews, whatever it is, those can be super powerful. The next section can flow into why this, if it's got a unique mechanism or a unique ingredient, why this is different and then how it works, you'll notice there's a how it works section in pretty much all e-com pages even if there is not like a complicated process to explain. There's going to be either a one, two, three step, just even if it's something as simple as a powder that you mix into water, it will be something like take one scoop, step one, take one scoop, measure out one scoop, step two, drop it in water or your favorite beverage, step three, enjoy this delicious refreshing drink and enjoy whatever benefits. It's very simple like that but what that does is it helps break up the flow for the reader and really help them sort of, I guess imagine in their minds how simple it is to use. So think of this as like the product demo section of how it works, however you can capture that in three, ideally three steps but sometimes you might have five steps depending on what it is, you want to do that here. So again, just some more prompts for you to follow on like how to lay these out. Even more reviews, this is where I would recommend you put video testimonials, don't necessarily put videos at the top because videos do slow down the flow of the page as far as the reader goes, like they're scanning through and you don't want them to stop too long to hit play and watch a video. So I would recommend you push that further down because you want them to consume some information first. And then this is where you might introduce the spokesperson for the brand or the authority behind it, you know, describe their experience, why they created this, why this is such a powerful solution for whatever problem they're trying to solve. This is where you would actually reveal the product. So if you notice the product isn't revealed at the top. A lot of e-com pages, you do see them at the top and I'll show you and the next example is one with the product placement at the top. This one is more of a curiosity lead.
